frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Neha, please, itna overthink mat kar. You're 28, it's not like you have endless options. And this 'all men are creeps' attitude? Seriously? Aise toh you'll never find anyone. Sab mard ek jaise nahi hote, yaar. We're also out there, trying to find good partners. Thoda trust karna seekho, itna paranoid rehna theek nahi hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Vikram dismisses his sister's implied wariness towards men as 'overthinking' and an 'all men are creeps' attitude. He uses the 'not all men' argument ('Sab mard ek jaise nahi hote') and challenges her rationality ('itna paranoid rehna theek nahi hai'), while demanding she 'trust' men, consistent with dismissing women's fears and feeling insulted on behalf of 'good guys'.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
